_comment_2: JSON does not support comments natively; underscored keys are convention-only.
_comment_3: After editing, restart Claude Code so it re-reads the settings.

hooks._comment: PreToolUse blocks via exit 2; PostToolUse is informational; Stop runs on conversation end.

Stop._comment: macOS notification on conversation end. Localise the text or remove this block on Linux.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Task complete" with title "Claude Code" sound name "Glass"'

extraKnownMarketplaces._comment: Each marketplace is a git repo with a .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. Add as needed.

enabledPlugins._comment_1: Set to true to enable; set the value to false (or remove the key) to disable.
enabledPlugins._comment_2: After a fresh install, also run: claude plugin install <name>@<marketplace>

_comment_mcp_overview: MCP servers come from THREE places — restore each one separately:
_comment_mcp_step_1: 1) ~/.mcp.json (user-scope MCPs running locally via npx). Restore with: cp personal/mcp.template.json ~/.mcp.json
_comment_mcp_step_2: 2) ~/.claude/settings.local.json (per-machine list of which MCPs are enabled). Restore with: echo '{"enabledMcpjsonServers":["context7","sequential-thinking"]}' > ~/.claude/settings.local.json
_comment_mcp_step_3: 3) MCPs that need API tokens (e.g. github) — install via: claude mcp add github -e GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN={{YOUR_GITHUB_PAT}} -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github
_comment_post_install: After settings.json + .mcp.json + settings.local.json are in place, install marketplace plugins:
